differences.title: 1. CORE CONCEPT
differences.leftDesc: State divided into independent, self-contained units called atoms.
differences.rightDesc: Single store holding state, updated via reducers.

differences.title: 2. API SIMPLICITY
differences.leftDesc: Defining atoms and selectors can require more initial setup.
differences.rightDesc: Extremely concise API, minimal code for common tasks.

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Updates only re-render components subscribed to the changed atom.
differences.rightDesc: Optimized re-renders based on state changes and selectors.

differences.title: 4. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Understanding atom/selector relationship takes a bit more effort.
differences.rightDesc: Hooks-based API is familiar and easy to grasp quickly.

differences.title: 5. EXTENSIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Selectors allow deriving state and managing complex dependencies.
differences.rightDesc: Supports middleware for logging, persistence, and async actions.

differences.title: 6. DEPENDENCY MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Selectors clearly define their dependencies on other atoms.
differences.rightDesc: Dependencies are inferred from the state accessed within selectors.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Strong adoption within the React ecosystem, actively developed.
differences.rightDesc: One of the most popular state management solutions for React.
